topic: sparkling wine pressure and mousse intensity

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A sparkling wine contains less dissolved carbon dioxide and develops pressure below approximately three atmospheres.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Lower pressure provides less force for carbon dioxide to leave solution when the bottle is opened and the wine is poured.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Effervescence feels gentler and the mousse is softer than in a wine at the five-to-six-atmosphere pressure typical of Champagne.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Effervescence feels gentler and the mousse is softer than in a wine at the five-to-six-atmosphere pressure typical of Champagne.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
